pairs.question: Who was Elihu Parsons Ingersoll and what were his significant contributions to academia?
pairs.answer: Elihu Parsons Ingersoll was an American pastor best known for his pioneering role in academia. In 1835, he became the first-ever Professor of Sacred Music at an American college, Oberlin College, marking a historic appointment in music education.

pairs.question: What are the key milestones in Elihu Parsons Ingersoll's education and early career?
pairs.answer: Ingersoll graduated from Yale College in 1832. He then studied at Auburn Theological Seminary for one year and Yale Divinity School for over a year before being ordained as a pastor of the Congregational Church in Woonsocket, Rhode Island, in December 1834.

pairs.question: Why did Elihu Parsons Ingersoll leave his position in Woonsocket, Rhode Island, and what did he do next?
pairs.answer: Ingersoll left his position in Woonsocket in November 1835 to accept a call to Oberlin College, where he spent nearly five years, partially serving as a professor of sacred music.

pairs.question: What challenges did Elihu Parsons Ingersoll face when trying to establish an institution in Michigan, and what did he do afterward?
pairs.answer: Ingersoll moved to Michigan in 1840 with plans to found an institution similar to Oberlin, but the financial crisis of 1837 thwarted his efforts. He then spent thirteen years working as a home missionary in Michigan, largely at his own expense, before becoming a pastor in Bloomington, Illinois, in 1853.

pairs.question: How did Elihu Parsons Ingersoll contribute to the anti-slavery cause in Kansas Territory?
pairs.answer: In April 1857, Ingersoll resigned from his position in Bloomington to support the anti-slavery movement in Kansas Territory. He worked without pay as a minister, primarily around Burlingame, until 1861.

pairs.question: What notable publication did Elihu Parsons Ingersoll release in 1886, and what is known about it?
pairs.answer: In 1886, Ingersoll published a book titled 'Lost Israel Found,' which is an 84-page, 12mo. volume.

pairs.question: How many children did Elihu Parsons Ingersoll have, and who were his spouses?
pairs.answer: Ingersoll had eight children with his second wife, Cathaiine, daughter of the Rev. Moses Gillett. His first wife was Louisa Perry, whom he married in 1835 and who died in 1836, leaving an infant son.
